content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward modules like ICM and RND can be embedded as dynamic curiosity gatekeepers in hybrid planning-learning systems, where they prioritize which unexplored state transitions get added to the planner’s explicit memory tree rather than rewarding the agent for every novel observation. This gated allocation fixes the common 'noisy novelty' trap of pure curiosity-driven RL in open environments, while also giving hybrid systems an automated way to expand their planning scope without manual reward engineering.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
